notes: Encoded participant identifier. This identifier is the same encoded id used in the main dataset.
Vaccination procedure carried out.
Vaccination procedure carried out (recoded). Provides a unified coding across the various sources for the procedure.
Vaccination product administered to participant.
Data source (data provider / region).
Date of vaccination.
A foreign key that refers to a standard Condition Concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies.
The end date for the Condition Era constructed from the individual instances of Condition Occurrences. It is the end date of the final continuously recorded instance of the Condition.
A unique identifier for each Condition Era.
The start date for the Condition Era constructed from the individual instances of Condition Occurrences. It is the start date of the very first chronologically recorded instance of the condition.
The number of individual Condition Occurrences used to construct the condition era.
A foreign key identifier to the Person who is experiencing the Condition during the Condition Era.
A foreign key that refers to a Standard Condition Concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies.
The date when the instance of the Condition is considered to have ended.
The date when the instance of the Condition is considered to have ended.
A unique identifier for each Condition Era.

The source code for the condition as it appears in the source data. This code is mapped to a standard condition concept in the Standardized Vocabularies and the original code is stored here for reference.
A foreign key to a Condition Concept that refers to the code used in the source.
The date when the instance of the Condition is recorded.
The date and time when the instance of the Condition is recorded.

A foreign key to the predefined Concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the source data from which the condition was recorded, the level of standardization, and the type of occurrence.
A foreign key identifier to the Person who is experiencing the Condition.
A foreign key to the Provider in the PROVIDER table who was responsible for capturing (diagnosing) the Condition.
The reason that the condition was no longer present, as indicated in the source data.
A foreign key to the predefined concept in the standard vocabulary reflecting the condition status.
A foreign key to the visit in the VISIT table during which the Condition was determined (diagnosed).
A foreign key referring to a standard concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies for conditions.
A foreign key to the concept that refers to the code used in the source. Note, this variable name is abbreviated to ensure it will be allowable across database platforms.

The source code for the cause of death as it appears in the source data. This code is mapped to a standard concept in the Standardized Vocabularies and the original code is, stored here for reference.
The date the person was deceased. If the precise date including day or month is not known or not allowed, December is used as the default month, and the last day of the month the default day.

The date and time the person was deceased. If the precise date including day or month is not known or not allowed, December is used as the default month, and the last day of the month the default day.
A foreign key referring to the predefined concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting how the death was represented in the source data.
A foreign key identifier to the deceased person.
A foreign key that refers to a Standard Concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies for the Device concept.
The date the Device or supply was removed from use.
The date and time the Device or supply was removed from use.
A system-generated unique identifier for each Device Exposure.
The date the Device or supply was applied or used.
The date and time the Device or supply was applied or used.
A foreign key to a Device Concept that refers to the code used in the source.
The source code for the Device as it appears in the source data. This code is mapped to a standard Device Concept in the Standardized Vocabularies and the original code is stored here for reference.

A foreign key to the predefined Concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the type of Device Exposure recorded. It indicates how the Device Exposure was represented in the source data.
A foreign key identifier to the Person who is subjected to the Device.
A foreign key to the provider in the PROVIDER table who initiated of administered the Device.
The number of individual Devices used for the exposure.
A UDI or equivalent identifying the instance of the Device used in the Person.
A foreign key to the visit in the VISIT table during which the device was used.
The end date for the drug era constructed from the individual instance of drug exposures. It is the end date of the final continuously recorded instance of utilization of a drug.
A unique identifier for each Dose Era.
The start date for the drug era constructed from the individual instances of drug exposures. It is the start date of the very first chronologically recorded instance of utilization of a drug.
The numeric value of the dose.
A foreign key that refers to a Standard Concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies for the active Ingredient Concept.
A foreign key identifier to the Person who is subjected to the drug during the drug era.
A foreign key that refers to a Standard Concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies for the unit concept.
A foreign key that refers to a Standard Concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies for the Ingredient Concept.
The end date for the drug era constructed from the individual instance of drug exposures. It is the end date of the final continuously recorded instance of utilization of a drug.
A unique identifier for each Drug Era.
The start date for the Drug Era constructed from the individual instances of Drug Exposures. It is the start date of the very first chronologically recorded instance of conutilization of a Drug.
The number of individual Drug Exposure occurrences used to construct the Drug Era.
A foreign key identifier to the Person who is subjected to the Drug during the Drug Era.
The number of days that are not covered by DRUG_EXPOSURE records that were used to make up the era record.
The number of days of supply of the medication as recorded in the original prescription or dispensing record.
The information about the dose unit as detailed in the source.
A foreign key that refers to a Standard Concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies for the Drug concept.
The end date for the current instance of Drug utilization. It is not available from all sources.
The end date and time for the current instance of Drug utilization. It is not available from all sources.
A system-generated unique identifier for each Drug utilization event.

The start date for the current instance of Drug utilization. Valid entries include a start date of a prescription, the date a prescription was filled, or the date on which a Drug administration procedure was recorded.

The start date and time for the current instance of Drug utilization. Valid entries include a start date of a prescription, the date a prescription was filled, or the date on which a Drug administration procedure was recorded.
A foreign key to a Drug Concept that refers to the code used in the source.
The source code for the Drug as it appears in the source data. This code is mapped to a Standard Drug concept in the Standardized Vocabularies and the original code is, stored here for reference.

A foreign key to the predefined Concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the type of Drug Exposure recorded. It indicates how the Drug Exposure was represented in the source data.
A foreign key identifier to the person who is subjected to the Drug. The demographic details of that person are stored in the person table.
An identifier assigned to a particular quantity or lot of Drug product from the manufacturer.
A foreign key to the provider in the provider table who initiated (prescribed or administered) the Drug Exposure.
The quantity of drug as recorded in the original prescription or dispensing record.
The number of refills after the initial prescription. The initial prescription is not counted, values start with 0.
A foreign key to a predefined concept in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the route of administration.
The information about the route of administration as detailed in the source.
The directions on the Drug prescription as recorded in the original prescription (and printed on the container) or dispensing record.
The reason the Drug was stopped. Reasons include regimen completed, changed, removed, etc.
A foreign key to the visit in the visit table during which the Drug Exposure was initiated.
A foreign key identifier to the Person about whom the measurement was recorded.
A foreign key to the standard measurement concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies.
The date of the Measurement.

The date and time of the Measurement. (Some database systems don't have a datatype of time. To accomodate all temporal analyses, datatype datetime can be used (combining measurement_date and measurement_time)Relevant Forum Discussion
A unique identifier for each Measurement.
A foreign key to a Concept in the Standard Vocabularies that refers to the code used in the source.
The Measurement name as it appears in the source data. This code is mapped to a Standard Concept in the Standardized Vocabularies and the original code is stored here for reference.
A foreign key to the predefined Concept in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the provenance from where the Measurement record was recorded.
A foreign key identifier to the predefined Concept in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the mathematical operator that is applied to the value_as_number. Operators are <, ?, =, ?, >.
A foreign key to the provider in the PROVIDER table who was responsible for initiating or obtaining the measurement.
The upper limit of the normal range of the Measurement. The upper range is assumed to be of the same unit of measure as the Measurement value.
The lower limit of the normal range of the Measurement result. The lower range is assumed to be of the same unit of measure as the Measurement value.
A foreign key to a Standard Concept ID of Measurement Units in the Standardized Vocabularies.
The source code for the unit as it appears in the source data. This code is mapped to a standard unit concept in the Standardized Vocabularies and the original code is stored here for reference.
A foreign key to a Measurement result represented as a Concept from the Standardized Vocabularies (e.g., positive/negative, present/absent, low/high, etc.).
A Measurement result where the result is expressed as a numeric value.
The source value associated with the content of the value_as_number or value_as_concept_id as stored in the source data.
A foreign key to the Visit in the VISIT_OCCURRENCE table during which the Measurement was recorded.
A foreign key identifier to the Person about whom the Note was recorded.
A foreign key to the predefined Concept in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the note character encoding type.
A foreign key to the predefined Concept in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the language of the note.
A foreign key to the predefined Concept in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the HL7 LOINC Document Type Vocabulary classification of the note.
The date the note was recorded.
The date and time the note was recorded.
A unique identifier for each note.
The content of the Note.
The title of the Note as it appears in the source.
A foreign key to the predefined Concept in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the type, origin or provenance of the Note.
A foreign key to the Provider in the PROVIDER table who took the Note.
Foreign key to the Visit in the VISIT_OCCURRENCE table when the Note was taken.
A foreign key identifier to the Person about whom the observation was recorded.
A foreign key to the standard observation concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies.
The date of the observation.
The date and time of the observation.
A unique identifier for each observation.
A foreign key to a Concept that refers to the code used in the source.
The observation code as it appears in the source data. This code is mapped to a Standard Concept in the Standardized Vocabularies and the original code is, stored here for reference.
A foreign key to the predefined concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the type of the observation.
A foreign key to the provider in the PROVIDER table who was responsible for making the observation.
A foreign key to a Standard Concept ID for a qualifier (e.g., severity of drug-drug interaction alert)
The source value associated with a qualifier to characterize the observation
A foreign key to a Standard Concept ID of measurement units in the Standardized Vocabularies.
The source code for the unit as it appears in the source data. This code is mapped to a standard unit concept in the Standardized Vocabularies and the original code is, stored here for reference.

A foreign key to an observation result stored as a Concept ID. This is applicable to observations where the result can be expressed as a Standard Concept from the Standardized Vocabularies (e.g., positive/negative, present/absent, low/high, etc.).
The observation result stored as a number. This is applicable to observations where the result is expressed as a numeric value.
The observation result stored as a string. This is applicable to observations where the result is expressed as verbatim text.
A foreign key to the visit in the VISIT_OCCURRENCE table during which the observation was recorded.
A foreign key identifier to the person for whom the observation period is defined.
The end date of the observation period for which data are available from the data source.
A unique identifier for each observation period.
The start date of the observation period for which data are available from the data source.
A foreign key identifier to the predefined concept in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the source of the observation period information
The date and time of birth of the person.
A foreign key to the site of primary care in the care_site table, where the details of the care site are stored.
The day of the month of birth of the person. For data sources that provide the precise date of birth, the day is extracted and stored in this field.
A unique identifier for each person.
A foreign key that refers to the standard concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies for the ethnicity of the person.
A foreign key to the ethnicity concept that refers to the code used in the source.

The source code for the ethnicity of the person as it appears in the source data. The person ethnicity is mapped to a standard ethnicity concept in the Standardized Vocabularies and the original code is, stored here for reference.
A foreign key that refers to an identifier in the CONCEPT table for the unique gender of the person.
A foreign key to the gender concept that refers to the code used in the source.

The source code for the gender of the person as it appears in the source data. The person's gender is mapped to a standard gender concept in the Standardized Vocabularies; the original value is stored here for reference.
A foreign key to the place of residency for the person in the location table, where the detailed address information is stored.
The month of birth of the person. For data sources that provide the precise date of birth, the month is extracted and stored in this field.
An (encrypted) key derived from the person identifier in the source data. This is necessary when a use case requires a link back to the person data at the source dataset.
A foreign key to the primary care provider the person is seeing in the provider table.
A foreign key that refers to an identifier in the CONCEPT table for the unique race of the person.
A foreign key to the race concept that refers to the code used in the source.

The source code for the race of the person as it appears in the source data. The person race is mapped to a standard race concept in the Standardized Vocabularies and the original value is stored here for reference.

The year of birth of the person. For data sources with date of birth, the year is extracted. For data sources where the year of birth is not available, the approximate year of birth is derived based on any age group categorization available.
A foreign key identifier to the Person who is subjected to the Procedure.
A foreign key to a Standard Concept identifier for a modifier to the Procedure (e.g. bilateral)
The source code for the qualifier as it appears in the source data.
A foreign key that refers to a standard procedure Concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies.
The date on which the Procedure was performed.
The date and time on which the Procedure was performed.

A foreign key to a Procedure Concept that refers to the code used in the source.

The source code for the Procedure as it appears in the source data. This code is mapped to a standard procedure Concept in the Standardized Vocabularies and the original code is, stored here for reference. Procedure source codes are typically ICD-9-Proc, CPT-4, HCPCS or OPCS-4 codes.
A foreign key to the predefined Concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the type of source data from which the procedure record is derived.
A foreign key to the provider in the provider table who was responsible for carrying out the procedure.
The quantity of procedures ordered or administered.
A foreign key to the visit in the visit table during which the Procedure was carried out.

Data source. The data source is either E/W for England & Wales or SCOT for Scotland.
Encoded participant identifier. This identifier is the same encoded id used in the main dataset.
Instance index. A numerical index used to distinguish the cases where we have multiple death records for a participant.
Data format. The particular data format in which this record was received.
Array index. A numerical index which together with the eid & ins_index uniquely identifies this record, i.e. eid, ins_index & arr_index together form a primary key for this table.
Cause of death - ICD-10. Coded according to the International Classification of Diseases version 10 (ICD-10).
Encoded participant identifier. This identifier is the same encoded id used in the main dataset.
Instance index. A numerical index which together with the eid uniquely identifies the corresponding record in the main DEATH table.
Classification of cause of death. Whether the cause of death is the primary cause or a contributory cause.
Data provider. Whether the record originates from England (Vision), Scotland, England (TPP) or Wales.
Encoded participant identifier.
Date clinical code was entered. Special coded values are given in Coding 819.
Read v2 code. Read version 2 code.
CTV3 (Read v3) code. Clinical Terms Version 3 code.
Value recorded 1.
Value recorded 2.
Value recorded 3.
Data provider. Whether the record originates from England (Vision), Scotland, England (TPP) or Wales.
Deduction date. The date of a participant's removal from GP list. Special coded values are given in Coding 819.
Encoded participant identifier.
Registration date. Special coded values are given in Coding 819.
BNF code. British National Formulary code.
Data provider. Whether the record originates from England (Vision), Scotland, England (TPP) or Wales.
dm+d code. Dictionary of medicines and devices (dm+d) code.
Drug name.
Encoded participant identifier.
Date prescription was issued. Special coded values are given in Coding 819.
Quantity issued.
Read v2 code. Read version 2 code.
Date of admission to hospital.
Methods of admission to hospital. Method of admission to hospital identifies how a patient was admitted, e.g. an elective admission from a waiting list or an emergency admission, etc.
Methods of admission to hospital (recoded). A field providing a unified coding across the various sources for the admimeth field.
Sources of admission to hospital. Source of admission identifies whether a patient came to the hospital from their usual place of residence or elsewhere.
Sources of admission to hospital (recoded). A field providing a unified coding across the various sources for the admisorc field.
Duration of episode within HES data year.
Carer support indicator. Carer support indicator identifies whether a carer is available to the patient at their usual place of residence.
Administrative and legal status of patient. Administrative and legal status identifies the type of patient and whether they have been formally detained under the Mental Health Act or not.
Patient classification on admission. Patient classification on admission identifies the type of patient, e.g. whether they are a regular attender or a maternity admission, etc.
Patient classification on admission (recoded). A field providing a unified coding across the various sources for the classpat field.
Date of discharge from hospital.

Destination on discharge from hospital. Destination on discharge identifies whether a patient was due to return to their usual place of residence on leaving hospital, or whether they were due to go elsewhere.
Destination on discharge from hospital (recoded). A field providing a unified coding across the various sources for the disdest field.

Method of discharge from hospital. Method of discharge from hospital identifies how a patient was discharged i.e. whether this was on clinical advice, the patient died in hospital or the patient discharged themselves.
Method of discharge from hospital (recoded). A field providing a unified coding across the various sources for the dismeth field.
Inpatient record origin. Either HES (England), PEDW (Wales) or SMR (Scotland). This can affect the coding used for some data fields.
Encoded participant identifier. This identifier is the same encoded id used in the main dataset.
Date of decision to admit to hospital. Not available for dsource='PEDW', or for source = 68 (one of the formats for dsource='SMR').
Waiting time for hospital admission. Not available for dsource='PEDW', or for source = 68 (one of the formats for dsource='SMR').
Duration of episode. The difference in days between the episode start date and the episode end date, where both are given.
Episode end date.
Episode order. The position of this episode within a spell (starting at 1).
Episode start date.
Episode status. Whether the episode had finished before the end of the HES data-year. An unfinished episode would be replaced by its finished version when the data is next updated.
Episode type. Hospital episode type identifies whether the period of care in hospital was, for example, a general episode, a maternity episode or a psychiatric episode.
First regular day or night of admission to hosp.
PCT where patients GP was registered. The PCT code identifies the administrative body where the patients GP practice is registered.

Instance index. A numerical index which together with the eid uniquely identifies this record, i.e. eid & ins_index together form a primary key for this table. The ins_index will not generally index records in chronological order, and may contain gaps in the numbering if records were added and then later removed for some reason.
Intended management of patient. The intended management of a patient is their planned duration of hospital stay, i.e. whether an overnight stay was anticipated or not.
Intended management of patient (recoded). A field providing a unified coding across the various sources for the intmanag field.
Main speciality of consultant. The main speciality of the consultant is the specialism under which the consultant is contracted e.g. cardiology or geriatric medicine, etc.
Main speciality of consultant (recoded). A field providing a unified coding across the various sources for the mainspef field.
Operation status. Whether an operation/procedure was carried out as part of this episode.
Current PCT responsible for patient. The PCT code identifies the administrative body responsible for the patient.
Inpatient record format. A finer split of the format of data released from each dsource above. This can also affect the coding used for some data fields.
Beginning of spell indicator. An indicator that this episode is the first in a spell. Only present in English inpatient data (dsource = HES).
Duration of spell.
End of spell indicator. An indicator that this episode is the last in a spell. Only present in English inpatient data (dsource = HES).
Spell index. A numerical index indicating which spell this episode belongs to (where possible). Spells will not generally be indexed in chronological order.
Spell sequence. A numerical index, starting from 0, giving the chronological position within its spell that this episode occupies (where possible).
Treatment speciality of consultant. The treatment speciality of the consultant is the specialism under which the consultant was working e.g. cardiology or geriatric medicine, etc.
Treatment speciality of consultant (recoded). A field providing a unified coding across the various sources for the tretspef field.
Advanced cardiovascular support days. Number of days of advanced cardiovascular support. For the definition of advanced cardiovascular support see Resource 141140 or the NHS CC Data Dictionary.
Advanced respiratory support days. Number of days of advanced respiratory support. For the definition of advanced respiratory support see Resource 141140 or the NHS CC Data Dictionary.
Array index. A numerical index which together with the eid and ins_index uniquely identifies this record, i.e. eid, ins_index and arr_index together form a primary key for this table.
Basic cardiovascular support days. Number of days of basic cardiovascular support.  For the definition of basic cardiovascular support see Resource 141140 or the NHS CC Data Dictionary.
Basic respiratory support days. Number of days of basic respiratory support. For the definition of basic respiratory support see Resource 141140 or the NHS CC Data Dictionary.
Source of admission to critical care. Whether the patient originated from the same or a different NHS hospital, an independent hospital provider, a non-hospital UK source, or a non-UK source.

Critical care admission type. Whether a critical care period was initiated as a result of a non-emergency treatment plan, for example, for non-elective surgery. This relates only to the period of critical care and not to the nature of the hospital admission.
Critical care APC relationship id. A comparison of the dates of the critical care period and those of the associated admitted patient care episode.
Date of discharge from critical care. Discharge date from unit if alive, date of death or date of declaration of brain stem death.

Critical care discharge destination. Whether the patient was discharged to the same or a different NHS hospital, an independent hospital provider, a non-hospital UK destination or to a non-UK source, or whether the patient died in the unit.
Critical care discharge location. The principal location that the patient was discharged to for further care at the end of the critical care period.
Ready-to-discharge date from critical care. The date on which the patient has been declared clinically ready for discharge or transfer from the clinical care period.

Critical care discharge status. The patient status at time of discharge, for example whether they were fully ready for discharge, whether their discharge was early or delayed, whether they self-discharged, or whether they died rather than being discharged (and their organ-donor status if so).
Critical care level 2 days. Total calendar days during which level 2 care alone was provided during the period.
Critical care level 3 days. Total calendar days during which level 3 care alone was provided during the period.
Location of the source of admission. The type of location the patient was in prior to the start of the critical care period.
Date on which the critical care period started.
Critical care unit function. The type of area to which the patient was admitted during a critical care period. This is the principal clinical service provided within the ward.
Dermatological support days. Number of days of dermatological support. For the definition of dermatological support see Resource 141140 or the NHS CC Data Dictionary.
Critical care record origin. Currently only HES (England).
Encoded participant identifier. This identifier is the same encoded id used in the main dataset.
Gastrointestinal support days. Number of days of gastrointestinal support. For the definition of gastrointestinal support see Resource 141140 or the NHS CC Data Dictionary.
Instance index. A numerical index which together with the eid uniquely identifies the corresponding record in the main HESIN table.
Liver support days. Number of days of liver support. For the definition of liver support see Resource 141140 or the NHS CC Data Dictionary.
Neurological support days. Number of days of neurological support.  For the definition of neurological support see Resource 141140 or the NHS CC Data Dictionary.

Organ support maximum. Maximum number of organ systems supported at any one time, at any point in the critical care period (note that both basic and advanced categories cannot be counted at the same time). This may not be the same as the total number of organs supported throughout the critical care admission. The minimum for this variable is 0 and the maximum is 7.
Renal support days. Number of days of renal support in the context of critical illness. For the definition of renal support see Resource 141140 or the NHS CC Data Dictionary.
Critical care record format. A finer split of the format of data released from each dsource. Currently only 60.
Critical care unit bed configuration. The composition of bed types for the critical care unit.
Array index. A numerical index which together with the eid & ins_index uniquely identifies this record, i.e. eid, ins_index & arr_index together form a primary key for this table.

Resuscitation method. Resuscitation method identifies whether any procedures were required to get the baby breathing normally; for example: positive pressure (nil/by mask/by endotracheal tube) and whether drugs were administered.
Birth order. No longer available for source=71 (April 2018) onwards.
Status of baby at birth. Status baby identifies whether the baby was born alive or stillborn (ante-partum or intra-partum). No longer available for source=71 (April 2018) onwards.
Weight of baby. No longer available for source=71 (April 2018) onwards.
Delivery method. Delivery method identifies the way in which the baby was born; for example: spontaneous, forceps, breech or caesarean section.
Delivery place. Delivery place identifies where the birth took place; for example: NHS hospital (with ward information) or at a domestic address.
Status of person conducting delivery. Status of person conducting delivery identifies whether a doctor, GP or midwife was present at the birth.
Encoded participant identifier. This identifier is the same encoded id used in the main dataset.
Number of weeks of gestation.
Instance index. A numerical index which together with the eid uniquely identifies the corresponding record in the main HESIN table and the HESIN_MATERNITY table.
Sex of baby. Sex of baby determines whether the baby was male, female or indeterminate. No longer available for source=71  (April 2018) onwards.
Array index. A numerical index which together with the eid & ins_index uniquely identifies this record, i.e. eid, ins_index & arr_index together form a primary key for this table.
Diagnoses - ICD10. Diagnoses are coded according to the International Classification of Diseases version-10 (ICD 10).
Diagnoses - ICD10 addendum.
Diagnoses - ICD9. Diagnoses are coded according to the International Classification of Diseases version-9 (ICD 9).
Diagnoses - ICD9 addendum.
Encoded participant identifier. This identifier is the same encoded id used in the main dataset.
Instance index. A numerical index which together with the eid uniquely identifies the corresponding record in the main HESIN table.

Classification of diagnosis. The primary diagnosis is the main condition treated or investigated during the relevant episode. A secondary diagnosis is a clinically relevant contributory factor or issue that impacts on the primary diagnosis (including chronic conditions). An external cause is an environmental event causing injury, poisoning or other type of adverse effect.
Gestation period at first antenatal assessment.
Date of first antenatal assessment.
Antenatal days of stay.

Reason for changing delivery place. Reason for changing delivery place identifies whether a decision was made during pregnancy or labour, and the reasons for the change (address change, clinical or other reasons).
Intended delivery place. Intended delivery place identifies where the birth was planned to take place; for example: NHS hospital (with ward information) or at a domestic address.
Delivery onset method. Delivery onset method identifies whether the onset of labour was spontaneous or clinically induced.
Anaesthetic delivered post delivery. This field identifies, for example, whether a general anaesthetic or an epidural was administered after labour or not.
Anaesthetic administered during delivery. This field identifies, for example, whether a general anaesthetic or an epidural was administered either before or during labour.
Encoded participant identifier. This identifier is the same encoded id used in the main dataset.

Instance index. A numerical index which together with the eid uniquely identifies this record, i.e. eid & ins_index together form a primary key for this table. The ins_index will not generally index records in chronological order. The record in HESIN corresponding to a particular HESIN_MATERNITY record will have the same eid & ins_index.
Mother age on date of delivery.
Number of babies delivered from single pregnancy. No longer available for source=71 (April 2018) onwards.
Number of previous pregnancies.
Postnatal stay.
Array index. A numerical index which together with the eid & ins_index uniquely identifies this record, i.e. eid, ins_index & arr_index together form a primary key for this table.
Encoded participant identifier. This identifier is the same encoded id used in the main dataset.
Instance index. A numerical index which together with the eid uniquely identifies the corresponding record in the main HESIN table.
Classification of operation/procedure.
Date of operation.
Operative procedures - OPCS3. Operative procedures are coded according to the Office of Population Censuses and Surveys Classification of Interventions and Procedures, version 3 (OPCS-3).
Operative Procedures - OPCS3 addendum.
Operative procedures - OPCS4. Operative procedures are coded according to the Office of Population Censuses and Surveys Classification of Interventions and Procedures, version 4 (OPCS-4).
Operative Procedures - OPCS4 addendum.
Duration of post-operative stay.
Duration of pre-operative stay.
History of psychiatric care on admission. History of psychiatric care identifies whether a patient has previously had a psychiatric episode in hospital or not.
History of psychiatric care on admission (recoded). History of psychiatric care identifies whether a patient has previously had a psychiatric episode in hospital or not.
Detention category. Detention category identifies the legislation under which the patient was detained.
Detention category (recoded). Detention category identifies the legislation under which the patient was detained.
Detention date.
Encoded participant identifier. This identifier is the same encoded id used in the main dataset.

Instance index. A numerical index which together with the eid uniquely identifies this record, i.e. eid & ins_index together form a primary key for this table. The ins_index will not generally index records in chronological order. The record in HESIN corresponding to a particular HESIN_PSYCH record will have the same eid & ins_index.
Legal status of patient. Legal status determines whether the patient was formally or informally detained and the relevant section of the Mental Health Act under which they were detained.
Mental category of patient. The mental categories are in accordance with the Mental Health Act 1983; for example, mental illness, (severe) mental impairment, psychopathic disorder.

Instance index. A numerical index used to distinguish cases.
Plate limit of detection NPX for an assay.
Shipment plate containing sample aliquot used for Olink Explore analysis.
Encoded ID linking to UniProt Gene name and Protein name.
Normalized Protien Expression result.
Position of well containing sample aliquot used for Olink Explore analysis.
